feature_name: Dual-mode operation (Root Complex or End Point)
description: Controller supports RC or EP mode; selection via bootstrap or software override. RC initiates configuration cycles and manages downstream enumeration; EP exposes Type 0 configuration and responds to host. Finalize mode selection before enabling LTSSM to avoid link mis-training.
dependencies: PSC power domain enabled; DEVSTAT PCIe enabled; valid SerDes reference clock; configure before LTSSM enable

description: Supports 2.5 GT/s (Gen1) and 5.0 GT/s (Gen2). Configure via Link Control 2 or Port Logic GEN2 registers, then retrain. Verify negotiated speed post-training and apply required equalization settings for Gen2 stability.
dependencies: Stable reference clock; SerDes PLL locked; common-clock and SSC settings consistent with partner

feature_name: x1/x2 link width with lane reversal and polarity inversion
description: Operates with x1 or x2 link widths. Automatic lane reversal and RX polarity inversion are supported. Controller may down-train width based on partner capabilities or signal integrity.
dependencies: SerDes lane enables configured; partner supports desired width; PLL locked

description: Outbound translation regions map system addresses into PCIe memory space. Program indices, offsets, and size per region, then enable after link-up to prevent target errors.
dependencies: OB region tables programmed; link up; non-cacheable attributes set for MMIO

description: Inbound regions translate PCIe BAR hits into system address space for EP host access or peer-to-peer writes. Configure BAR match, start address, and offset per region.
dependencies: BAR sizing aligned with host enumeration; IB regions enabled; MMIO attributes

description: Application endianness selectable to match system architecture. Configure byte swapping before enabling translators and traffic to avoid data corruption on inbound/outbound paths.
dependencies: ENDIAN register programmed prior to OB/IB/DMA activity

feature_name: TLP attributes: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop
description: Outbound TLPs can request Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop. Use where interconnect and cache policy permit to improve throughput; validate effects on coherency-sensitive paths.
dependencies: System memory ordering rules and cache policy allow attributes; performance validation

description: Supports legacy INTA–INTD signaling mapped to SoC interrupts. Use proper enable/mask sequencing, W1C semantics for status, and write EOI to complete servicing.
dependencies: SoC interrupt routing configured; status/enable registers managed; EOI writes

description: As RC, receives MSIs from endpoints. Vectors are grouped in raw/status/enable sets; clear with W1C. Configure endpoint MSI capability during enumeration.
dependencies: Endpoint MSI enabled during enumeration; SoC IRQ mapping complete

description: As EP, generates MSI to host by programming local MSI target and writing vector. Host must allocate and enable vectors; fall back to INTx if MSI disabled.
dependencies: MSI capability configured; host assigned vectors; BAR/IB mappings valid

description: Supports device power management and ASPM L0s/L1 states. Enter/exit with quiesced traffic to minimize latency impact. L2 state is not supported on this controller.
dependencies: Stable link; partner ASPM support; follow timing constraints

description: Dedicated interrupts for PME, turn-off acknowledge, and reset events. Must be enabled and serviced promptly to ensure orderly power and reset sequencing.
dependencies: SoC IRQ mapping; correct status clear order; debounce where needed

description: AER logs correctable and uncorrectable errors with severity and first error pointer. ECRC generation/checking detects TLP corruption; disable when peers lack support.
dependencies: AER and Root Error Command enabled; error MSI/INTx routed; log before clearing

feature_name: SerDes PLL configuration and lock detection
description: Program SerDes PLL and per-lane rates, then verify lock prior to link training. Lane status and PCS diagnostics assist in SI bring-up and troubleshooting.
dependencies: Stable external refclk; proper PLL parameters; observed lock wait

feature_name: Link training control and LTSSM monitoring
description: Gate training with LTSSM_EN; start negotiation only after configuration is complete. Monitor LTSSM to L0 for success and handle error or recovery states as needed.
dependencies: SerDes locked; translators and interrupts configured; debug registers readable

description: Issue downstream hot reset after quiescing traffic to recover misbehaving endpoints. Observe required delays and re-enumerate affected devices upon link recovery.
dependencies: RC role; traffic flushed; reset timing honored

description: Perform Type 0/1 configuration and IO cycles through dedicated windows. Only 32-bit, single, non-burst, non-cacheable accesses are permitted and only when link is up.
dependencies: CFG_SETUP indices programmed; IOBASE set; target presence verified

description: Independently enable/disable outbound and inbound translators to stage bring-up and isolate faults. Disable regions before remapping to avoid stray accesses.
dependencies: Valid OB/IB tables; known link state; handlers ready for faults

description: Per-lane PCS/SerDes controls expose status and limited tuning for lab use. Apply cautiously and revert to defaults for production.
dependencies: SerDes powered; privileged access; board SI validated

feature_name: 128-bit VBUSM interfaces and MMIO access rules
description: Wide internal buses support high throughput, but controller special windows require 32-bit, single, non-burst, strongly-ordered accesses to ensure correctness.
dependencies: MMIO attributes set appropriately; helpers enforce access width

description: Enable common-clock when endpoints share refclk; optionally enable SSC to reduce EMI if both sides support. Mismatch can degrade Gen2 performance.
dependencies: Board clock topology known; Port Logic GEN2 configured

description: Power and clock PCIe domain, configure SerDes and endianness, set translators and interrupts with LTSSM disabled. Enable LTSSM, wait for L0, then enumerate and enable MSI on endpoints.
dependencies: PSC/DEVSTAT set; SerDes lock; retry and timeout policies in place

description: Configure BARs, capabilities, and inbound windows; enable LTSSM to link with host. After link, respond to host configuration and enable MSI for driver use.
dependencies: BAR sizes chosen; IB/OB programmed; SerDes locked

description: No hardware hot-plug controller and no L2 link state support. Software must avoid relying on these features and implement alternative recovery strategies.
dependencies: Design avoids hot-plug/L2 assumptions; platform-level handling

feature_name: Payload and read request size limits
description: Maximum outbound payload 128 B; inbound payload and read request up to 256 B. Program capabilities and negotiate MPS/MRRS within limits for stability.
dependencies: Enumeration code configures Device/Link Control fields

description: Configure master priority and privilege IDs to integrate with SoC QoS and security. Tune conservatively to prevent starvation and unintended privilege escalation.
dependencies: PRIORITY register policy; SoC QoS guidelines

description: Activity and diagnostic registers aid throughput tuning and fault isolation. Follow access rules and sample during stable link periods for consistency.
dependencies: Use non-burst 32-bit reads; minimize impact during sampling

description: I/O aperture is small and many modern endpoints lack I/O BARs. Prefer memory-mapped access and validate I/O usage only for legacy devices.
dependencies: RC mode; endpoints advertise I/O BARs; CFG programmed

description: Multiple virtual channels/traffic classes and function-level reset are not supported. Do not advertise or depend on these capabilities in software.
dependencies: Constrain capability advertisement; clear unsupported bits

description: PHY loopback supported for validation in RC mode; use to verify signal paths and link training in isolation. Disable for normal operation.
dependencies: SerDes configured; lab setup; link partner isolated

description: Detect link-down via interrupts or LTSSM state; recommended recovery is full PCIe subsystem reset and re-initialization to restore operation.
dependencies: Interrupt routing; reset controls available; re-init sequence

description: Supports PCIe message TLPs with filtering of posted, non-posted, and completions. Configure acceptance as required by platform messaging policies.
dependencies: Message control registers; platform policy defined
